title: Mortality rate, neonatal (per 1,000 live births)
definition: Neonatal mortality rate is the number of neonates dying before reaching 28 days of age, per 1,000 live births in a given year.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for neonatal mortality.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include South Sudan, Nigeria, Pakistan, while the lightest include Belarus, Montenegro, Estonia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the neonatal mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Neonatal mortality may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Infant mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Under-5 mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Human Capital Index can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: strong health metric, but it is extremely close to infant mortality and under-five mortality.
Expert-only if used at all; keep it out of ordinary Practice until mortality clusters are tuned.
